Melicope is a genus of flowering plants commonly known as the "lemonwoods" or "lemon trees". The health benefits of Melicope plants are not well-documented, but they are known to have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to their potential health benefits.

The bioactive constituents of Melicope plants include flavonoids, limonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. 

The most common herbal preparations made with Melicope plants are essential oils and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine and as natural remedies for various health conditions.
